mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy, which reduces the cost of capital and encourages investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as market participants anticipate economic recovery.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS has been cut from 5.25% in late 2007 to 2.28% by April 2008
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS has declined over the prior 3 months, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises significantly from its recent low, it signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate earnings expectations are revised downwards.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points from its 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE has risen from 4.4% in 2007 to 5.0% by April 2008
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or if the average return in triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates a broad-based weakening of consumer demand and investment, which directly impacts corporate revenues and profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < 0% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:Both retail sales and housing starts show signs of slowing or contraction in early 2008
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of periods where both retail sales and housing starts are contracting, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and increases uncertainty about future corporate earnings, prompting investors to demand a higher risk premium, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 213.743 in April 2008, indicating high inflation
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances during high inflation regimes, or if the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A significant contraction in industrial production indicates a slowdown in manufacturing and overall economic activity, signaling reduced corporate output and profitability, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO growth has been slowing in early 2008
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of periods following a significant contraction in industrial production, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which generally translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has shown overall growth trend despite recent slowdowns
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of slowing GDP growth and rising inflation creates a stagflationary environment, where corporate profits are squeezed by higher costs and weaker demand, leading to significant negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 2.0% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth has slowed and inflation is elevated in early 2008
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of periods identified as stagflationary by these conditions, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rebound in housing starts after a prolonged decline signals renewed confidence in the housing market and broader economic recovery, which typically precedes positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as investor sentiment improves.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average shows first positive month-over-month change after 6 consecutive months of decline
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts have been declining significantly in 2007-2008
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of periods where housing starts show an initial rebound after a prolonged decline, this hypothesis is rejected.
